MV
"I write the same coastline over and over, hoping one day to get the light exactly right."
The writer · Marina Vøllo

Letters from the long shore

Novelist, essayist, and lifelong islander. For thirty years I've written about distance — the kind the tide makes, and the kind we make ourselves. My new novel is out this spring.

The new novel

Salt & Starlight

Two islands, one tide, and a correspondence that spans forty years.

Read an extract
The work, year by year

A bibliography

  1. 2026
    Novel · 2026 Salt & Starlight

    Two islands, one tide, and a correspondence that spans forty years.

  2. 2022
    Essays · 2022 Letters to the Sea

    Fifteen years of dispatches from a writing desk that faces the water.

  3. 2018
    Novel · 2018 The Weight of Mornings

    A family, a farmhouse, and the slow arithmetic of who stays and who leaves.

  4. 2013
    Novel · 2013 A Cartography of Dreams

    The debut — part atlas, part confession, mapping the places we visit only while sleeping.

EF
Author in the spotlight

Eleanor Finch

Finch writes the sea the way others write people — patiently, and with a long memory. Across nine novels she has become one of our most quietly essential voices, returning again and again to the coast where she grew up.

Read her full story

In her own words

"A book is a letter you write to a stranger and trust the years to deliver."

Marina Vøllo, from her notebooks

From my readers

Letters I keep
